file_insights.insights: Wave 4 dispatcher state updated with timestamp progression (13:08:05 → 14:46:17). Spec completion list expanded from [005-008] to [005-011], indicating successful spec materialization. Spec 010 appears in both 'completed' and 'failed' arrays, suggesting a retry/rerun scenario.

Spec 011 (fix-ci) contains complete QA rejection → fix cycle documentation. FIX_SUMMARY.md documents 4 issues fixed (clippy error flags, format checks, toolchain components, rust cache). QA_FIX_REQUEST.md captures original rejection with problem/fix/verification triples. Shows automated QA feedback loop in action.

Specs maintain structured metadata: context.json lists affected files by category (github_workflows, readme), implementation_plan.json tracks phases/subtasks, build-progress.txt documents sequential workflow execution. Session 1 performed investigation; later sessions implemented fixes. Pattern shows layered planning → execution → QA → fix cycle.

Each spec maintains persistent session history (6 session files captured for spec 011, 8 for spec 012). attempt_history.json tracks multiple tries. This enables cross-session context preservation and retry logic in orchestrator.
Spec 012 (dogfood-seed) shows parallel tracking with similar infrastructure to 011. Multiple session_insights (002-008) indicate ongoing execution across multiple agent sessions.

patterns_discovered.name: Wave-Based Orchestration
patterns_discovered.description: Dispatcher runs wave 4 with atomic state transitions. Specs move through state array as they complete. Timestamp tracking in dispatch-lock.json correlates with wave start time.

patterns_discovered.name: QA Rejection → Fix → Re-validation Cycle
patterns_discovered.description: Spec contains QA_FIX_REQUEST.md documenting rejection, FIX_SUMMARY.md documenting fixes, and local `make check` verification before re-submission. Enables automated QA loop with human-readable feedback.

patterns_discovered.name: Session-Scoped Memory Isolation
patterns_discovered.description: Each spec allocates numbered session directories (session_001.json, session_002.json, etc). Allows multi-agent parallel work with isolated context. Enables session-number tracking for debuggability.

patterns_discovered.name: Automated Report Generation
patterns_discovered.description: Specs auto-generate FIX_SUMMARY.md, MANUAL_TEST_PLAN.md, QA_FIX_REQUEST.md. Suggests templated documentation system. FIX_SUMMARY contains formatted lists with checkmarks (✅).

patterns_discovered.name: Layered Planning Architecture
patterns_discovered.description: Specs contain context.json (file mapping), implementation_plan.json (phases/subtasks), build-progress.txt (narrative progress). Three-layer abstraction: metadata → plan → execution log.

gotchas_discovered.impact: State ambiguity — unclear if spec should be retried, reported as failed, or marked for manual inspection. Current dual-state may confuse orchestrator retry logic.
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: Clarify state machine semantics: single SSOT state vs. side-effect tracking. Consider separate 'requires_rerun' array if retry scenario.

gotchas_discovered.issue: Timestamp gap in dispatch-lock (38 minutes between sessions)
gotchas_discovered.impact: Long pause between wave 4 dispatch invocations. Could indicate agent session timeout, manual intervention, or sequential wave batching. Not necessarily a problem, but unaccounted time.
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: Log dispatch invocation trigger (manual, timer, event) to correlate with timestamp gaps.

gotchas_discovered.issue: QA_FIX_REQUEST.md generated after rejection, not before implementation
gotchas_discovered.impact: Documentation lag — fix request document created retroactively rather than as spec input. Suggests QA feedback → document generation pipeline, not feed-forward.
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: If QA rejection is expected workflow, automate QA_FIX_REQUEST generation at rejection time, not at fix time. Enables pre-fix agent access to requirements.

gotchas_discovered.issue: MANUAL_TEST_PLAN.md is template-only, no actual test cases
gotchas_discovered.impact: QA test plan generated from template with unchecked checkboxes. Likely not executed unless QA agent fills it in manually. Could lead to false 'ready' signals.
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: Either auto-generate test cases from spec, or track test plan as 'awaiting QA', not 'complete'.

gotchas_discovered.issue: research/vendor/bolt.diy in file changes but no diff shown
gotchas_discovered.impact: Vendor directory modified but git diff truncated. Unknown what vendor code changed — could be dependency update, fetch, or corruption.
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: Verify bolt.diy is intentionally tracked and inspect separately with `git diff -- research/vendor/bolt.diy`.

approach_outcome.description: Verify all cards created and listed
deliverables: Dispatch state updated: specs 010, 011 added to completed list
Spec 011 (fix-ci) fully documented with 4 issues fixed + QA rejection → fix cycle recorded
Spec 012 (dogfood-seed) state tracked across 8 session snapshots
Wave 4 dispatcher confirmed running with persistent state files
verification_performed: Cards materialized in dispatch-state.json (specs 005-011 now listed)
Timestamp progression in dispatch-lock.json (38-minute gap between invocations)
Multiple spec 011 sessions completed (6 documented), multiple spec 012 sessions ongoing (8 documented)
approach_outcome.execution_flow: Orchestrator: wave 4 dispatch → enumerate specs → materialize state → auto-generate documentation → record session insights. Linear execution with persistent state machine.

recommendations.action: Define authoritative state for specs: Replace dual-state (spec 010 in both 'completed' and 'failed') with explicit enum: PENDING | RUNNING | COMPLETED | FAILED | NEEDS_RERUN. Update dispatch-state.json to use single-value per spec.

Reverse QA feedback direction: Generate QA_FIX_REQUEST.md at rejection time (make it a spec input), not retroactively. Enable fix agent to see requirements before implementing. Track feedback as 'issues_to_fix' in implementation_plan.json.

Replace MANUAL_TEST_PLAN.md template with generated test cases from spec acceptance criteria. If spec has AC 'make check passes', auto-generate test case 'Run make check and verify exit 0'. Mark test plan as 'pending_execution', not 'generated'.
Log trigger event for each dispatch-lock.json update. Add 'trigger_reason' field (TIMER | MANUAL | EVENT) to correlate 38-minute gaps with orchestrator behavior.

Verify research/vendor/bolt.diy is intentional. If external dependency, move to separate lockfile (e.g., vendor.lock.json) to prevent accidental commits. If development vendor code, document in CONTRIBUTING.md.
